proven.claim: PROVEN: available >= 0 at line 11, given db.getStock guarantees a non-negative return.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVEN: available >= 0 at line 11, given db.getStock guarantees a non-negative return.
; Strategy: precondition + line-10 transition + negated property → expect unsat.
(declare-const getStock_result Int)
(declare-const available Int)
; Precondition: db.getStock contract — returns non-negative stock level
(assert (>= getStock_result 0))
; Transition (line 10): available = db.getStock(productId)
(assert (= available getStock_result))
; Negated property: available < 0 at line 11
(assert (< available 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — available >= 0 is proven IF the caller honours the non-negativity contract.
; If unsat: the property holds conditionally on that contract.

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: checkAvailability reads db.getStock; reserveStock writes
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: checkAvailability reads db.getStock; reserveStock writes
; db.setAvailable (a different field). db.setStock is never called in this module.
; After a reservation executes, getStock is stale — the log over-reports availability.
(declare-const stock_initial Int)        ; db.getStock(productId) — never mutated in this module
(declare-const avail_initial Int)        ; db.getAvailable(productId) before reserveStock
(declare-const quantity Int)             ; quantity passed to reserveStock
(declare-const avail_after_reserve Int)  ; db.getAvailable after reserveStock line 21
(declare-const available_at_line11 Int)  ; value of `available` at target line 11
; Even if stock and available start equal (most generous assumption):
(assert (= stock_initial avail_initial))
(assert (>= avail_initial 0))
(assert (> quantity 0))
(assert (<= quantity avail_initial))     ; reservation is otherwise valid
; Transition — reserveStock line 21: db.setAvailable(productId, available - quantity)
(assert (= avail_after_reserve (- avail_initial quantity)))
; checkAvailability line 10: available = db.getStock(productId)
; db.setStock is never called anywhere in this module → getStock unchanged
(assert (= available_at_line11 stock_initial))
; Violation: log reports more units than are actually available post-reservation
(assert (> available_at_line11 avail_after_reserve))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — witness e.g. avail_initial=10, quantity=3 →
;   avail_after_reserve=7, available_at_line11=10, 10 > 7.
; checkAvailability silently over-reports availability after every reservation.

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: reserveStock does not validate quantity <= available.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: reserveStock does not validate quantity <= available.
; db.setAvailable(productId, available - quantity) executes unconditionally.
; If getStock and getAvailable share the same backing store (reasonable expectation),
; a subsequent call to checkAvailability logs a negative available count.
(declare-const avail_before Int)       ; db.getAvailable(productId) before reserveStock
(declare-const quantity Int)           ; caller-supplied — no validation in reserveStock
(declare-const avail_after Int)        ; state written by reserveStock line 21
(declare-const available_line11 Int)   ; `available` at target line 11 via getStock
; Domain: valid non-negative stock, positive quantity
(assert (>= avail_before 0))
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Transition — reserveStock line 21 (no guard on quantity <= avail_before):
(assert (= avail_after (- avail_before quantity)))
; If getStock reflects the same backing store as getAvailable:
(assert (= available_line11 avail_after))
; Precondition that reserveStock fails to enforce:
(assert (> quantity avail_before))
; Violation: negative available reported by the log at line 11
(assert (< available_line11 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — witness: avail_before=3, quantity=5 →
;   avail_after=-2 → available_line11=-2.
; The log emits "Stock check for X: -2 available"; checkAvailability returns -2.

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: The db stub returns 0 unconditionally for every productId.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: The db stub returns 0 unconditionally for every productId.
; The log at line 11 always emits "0 available" masking any real stock state.
; A caller seeing 0 cannot distinguish "genuinely out of stock" from "stub not wired up".
(declare-const stub_return Int)
(declare-const real_stock Int)        ; what an actual DB would hold
(declare-const available Int)
; Stub transition (line 10): db.getStock = (_) => 0
(assert (= stub_return 0))
(assert (= available stub_return))
; Real stock could be positive — the stub ignores it entirely
(assert (> real_stock 0))
; Violation: available reported at line 11 does not equal real_stock
(assert (not (= available real_stock)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — for any real_stock > 0, available = 0 at line 11.
; checkAvailability always returns 0, permanently misreporting every product's stock.

proven.claim: PROVE: With the stub db, every positive-quantity reservation will store negative available.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; PROVE: With the stub db, every positive-quantity reservation will store negative available.
; db.getAvailable and db.getReserved both unconditionally return 0 (see stub definition).
; Negated property: new_available >= 0. Expect unsat.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Transition: db.getAvailable stub returns 0 unconditionally
(assert (= available 0))
; Transition: db.getReserved stub returns 0 unconditionally
(assert (= reserved 0))
; Caller supplies any positive quantity (reasonable for a reservation)
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Transition: new_available = available - quantity, computed at line 23 / applied at line 20
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
; Negate property: new_available >= 0
(assert (>= new_available 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat -- the stub guarantees new_available = 0 - quantity < 0 for any quantity > 0

proven.claim: PROVE: The `available` and `reserved` logged at line 18 cannot equal the post-mutation
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: The `available` and `reserved` logged at line 18 cannot equal the post-mutation
; values whenever quantity != 0, because setAvailable/setReserved are called after line 18.
; Negated property: available = new_available despite quantity != 0. Expect unsat.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Transition: setAvailable stores available - quantity (line 20, after the log)
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
; Precondition for negation: a non-zero reservation
(assert (not (= quantity 0)))
; Negate: the logged value equals the post-mutation value
(assert (= available new_available))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat -- available = available - quantity implies quantity = 0, contradicting the premise

violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: reserveStock performs no validation before line 18.
; setAvailable(productId, available - quantity) on line 20 will write a negative value
; whenever quantity > available. Expect sat.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Transition: available = db.getAvailable(productId) -- assume domain invariant >= 0
(assert (>= available 0))
; No guard: quantity is caller-supplied, no check before the log at line 18
; Violation scenario: caller passes quantity larger than available stock
(assert (> quantity available))
; Transition: new_available = available - quantity (line 20 / line 23)
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))

violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: TypeScript `number` admits negative values. No guard exists before
; line 18. A negative quantity makes available INCREASE and reserved DECREASE --
; the opposite of what reserveStock is supposed to do. Expect sat.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transition: available = db.getAvailable(productId)
(assert (>= available 0))
; Transition: reserved = db.getReserved(productId)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; No validation before line 18: caller passes negative quantity
(assert (< quantity 0))
; Transition: setAvailable stores available - (negative) = available + |quantity| (line 20)
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
; Transition: setReserved stores reserved + (negative) = reserved - |quantity| (line 21)
(assert (= new_reserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Violation: available grows via a reservation call
(assert (> new_available available))
; Violation: reserved shrinks via a reservation call
(assert (< new_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: reserveStock has no idempotency guard. Two calls for the same
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: reserveStock has no idempotency guard. Two calls for the same
; productId execute sequentially; the second call reads the diminished state written by
; the first and can over-reserve into negative stock. Expect sat.
(declare-const available_0 Int)
(declare-const reserved_0 Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const available_1 Int)
(declare-const reserved_1 Int)
(declare-const available_2 Int)
(declare-const reserved_2 Int)
; Initial state
(assert (>= available_0 0))
(assert (>= reserved_0 0))
; First call: preconditions happen to hold (quantity_1 <= available_0)
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (<= quantity_1 available_0))
; Transition: state after first call (lines 20-21 of first invocation)
(assert (= available_1 (- available_0 quantity_1)))
(assert (= reserved_1 (+ reserved_0 quantity_1)))
; Second call: no validation before its line 18 either; quantity_2 is positive
(assert (> quantity_2 0))
; Transition: second call reads available_1 (written by first call) then subtracts again
(assert (= available_2 (- available_1 quantity_2)))
(assert (= reserved_2 (+ reserved_1 quantity_2)))
; Violation: second call drives available below zero
(assert (< available_2 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat -- e.g. available_0=5, q1=3, available_1=2, q2=4 -> available_2=-2
; No guard anywhere prevents this; the log at line 18 of the second call will display
; available=2 but nothing stops quantity_2=4 from proceeding

proven.claim: PROVE: newAvailable at line 25 equals the value written by setAvailable at line 22.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: newAvailable at line 25 equals the value written by setAvailable at line 22.
; Assert transitions, negate equality, expect unsat.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_written_available Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
; L22: setAvailable writes (available - quantity)
(assert (= db_written_available (- available quantity)))
; L24: newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Negate: the logged value differs from what was written
(assert (not (= newAvailable db_written_available)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — the log is definitionally faithful to the DB write.
; Same expression is evaluated twice; they are equal by substitution.

proven.claim: PROVE: stock conservation holds at line 25.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: stock conservation holds at line 25.
; newAvailable + newReserved must equal available + reserved algebraically.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; L24 transitions
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Negate conservation: total changes
(assert (not (= (+ newAvailable newReserved) (+ available reserved))))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — (available - quantity) + (reserved + quantity) = available + reserved
; by integer arithmetic, always. Quantity cancels out.

proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2

; Two-item cart model. If Z3 returns unsat the identity holds unconditionally.
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const bp2 Real)
(declare-const qty2 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal2 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Transition: loop body — lineTotal = basePrice * quantity
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))

(assert (= orderTotal (+ lineTotal1 lineTotal2)))
; Negate: orderTotal differs from the algebraic sum
(assert (not (= orderTotal (+ (* bp1 qty1) (* bp2 qty2)))))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — orderTotal is exactly the sum of lineTotals; no rounding or omission

proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: refundAmount at line 37 equals the algebraic sum of all (basePrice_i * quantity_i)
; Strategy: model the two-iteration accumulation, negate the sum identity → expect unsat

(declare-const qty0 Real)
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const r0 Real)

(assert (= r0 (* bp0 qty0)))

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: refundAmount is recomputed from live CartItem fields at refund time.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: refundAmount is recomputed from live CartItem fields at refund time.
; CartItem.basePrice is a mutable TypeScript field — processRefund does not compare
; to what placeOrder charged. If basePrice changed between order and refund, the customer
; receives a refund for a different amount than they were charged.
; Transition modeled: placeOrder computes orderTotal, then basePrice is mutated, then
; processRefund computes refundAmount from the mutated value.
(declare-const base_price_at_order Real)
(declare-const base_price_at_refund Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const order_total Real)
(declare-const refund_amount Real)
; placeOrder's transition: orderTotal = basePrice_original * quantity
(assert (> base_price_at_order 0.0))
(assert (> quantity 0.0))
(assert (= order_total (* base_price_at_order quantity)))
; TypeScript CartItem fields are mutable — basePrice can change between calls
(assert (not (= base_price_at_refund base_price_at_order)))
(assert (> base_price_at_refund 0.0))
; processRefund's transition: refundAmount = basePrice_current * quantity
(assert (= refund_amount (* base_price_at_refund quantity)))
; Violation: refundAmount != orderTotal at line 43
(assert (not (= refund_amount order_total)))
(check-sat)
; sat → mutated basePrice means refundAmount diverges from original payment.
;        A discounted item could be refunded at full price (or vice versa).
